content:

Any thoughts appreciated. Thanks

title: Change Default Email Setting
creator: KevinGrey
content:
I have 2 email accounts on TB. Well, 3 if you count the Plusnet one being set up for IMAP and POP3. This email has been my main one forever, but Plusnet have sold it off to an independent company, which I've opted out of as it will become a paid service after 2 yrs. Both IMAP & POP 3 are set as my default email & I want to change it to my Yahoo email that I only set up on POP3.

My 1st Q. is: I can see the red delete button next to the default notice in Settings. Does this just stop the email being my default or will it delete the account entirely? I don't seem able to make the Yahoo account my default without undoing the default settings for Plusnet first (makes sense). But just want to be sure.

Q.2: Since setting up Yahoo email on TB, I noticed that while all my Inbox messages remain when downloaded on my PC, the Sent messages are being deleted to leave only the most recent (currently) 11 messages, like it is if I log in online to Yahoo webmail. Does this mean I need to configure it to IMAP as well to make sure nothing is deleted from my PC?

Eventually, I want to delete the Plusnet accounts entirely from TB. Although I am still receiving emails sent to this address, which is still quite useful, I cannot use it to send or reply to these emails. Also, since the sell off I've been receiving unprecedented amounts of spam/scam emails, especially to my iPhone (TB isn't set up on my phone).

title: I cannot send thunderbird Emails on either of my accounts
creator: contacts8
content: On 6th Feb 2026 a professional set up Thunderbird [149 I think] on my Desk top computer, running Windows 10, with 2 email addresses. Last week I found I could receive emails on both, but not send on either. I know the passwords for both the addresses, but even if I put them in when asked, I still could not send. Then I found I was not receiving e mails on either address as well. I am 87 and may have changed something important, in an effort to find a solution, as last Friday [27th March] thunderbird crashed. I sent the report to thunderbird. Yesterday I received the previous day's emails and current ones. Today [29th] I am receiving, but not able to send again, My email default is set as Thunderbird. For many years I was with Plusnet, but recently I had to transfer to Greenby. I checked their Web mail when I was not receiving emails and they were on their Server. I can make simple changes in Account Settings eg Port, Outgoing Server, Security.. Is there a simple solution or should I call the Professional again? Thank you.

title: メッセージを送信できませんでした　メールの送信中にエラーが発生しました　サーバーからの応答：Messenge deferred ダイアログ
creator: kalin334
content: 「メッセージを送信できませんでした　メールの送信中にエラーが発生しました　サーバーからの応答：Messenge deferred」 というダイアログ（アラート）が頻繁に出てくるので迷惑。 「サーバーからの応答：Messenge deferred」とは何か？ 反故の設定：STARTTLS、SMTPも含め、いろいろと解決策を摸索したが駄目だった。 TLS1.2に対応 送信時のポート:587

title: TB will not let me create, reply or forward an email
creator: g.lahey
content:
After many years of using TB without any problems on an older computer with Win 10 I suddenly have this confusing little hiccup. If I hit New Message, Reply or Forward a small rectangular box (about 8 x 100mm) appears with a shaded outline in the centre of the screen where the body of the email would go. There is also a black lock with a red line through it at the start of the "Subject" line of the blank email, I don't think I have seen it there before but I maybe wrong.

Clicking inside the box gives nil response except on the far right on the formerly black X that has changed to a white X with a red background when it just deletes the whole page. No amount of right or left clicking outside the box gives any response except for the "error ding sound" and the box jiggles. The box can be dragged to the edge of the screen without any change in responses.

All other email functions work normally, there are no problems with receiving, reading or deleting emails. I do not know how to find out what version of TB that I have installed. So to sum up if I hit New Message, Reply or Forward the only thing TB will let me do is delete the new blank email that I want to send (once it is completed). I have not got the foggiest idea of what to do to fix the problem.

title: If a Telecom uses real Email, i/.e. not WEBmail, and they use POP3...
creator: redacted
content:
Hello, and thanks for the read...

ISSUE: Every day I have to call the telecom ROGERS that took over for telecom SHAW, turn IMAP and Pop3 on 9 Email accounts (they lost 1). Sometimes the 1 to 2 hors chat w/ a human turned it back on will not last long.

DETAILS:

  1. I can receive outside Emails *because I can't send using Shaw/Rogers, checking for Email, just hangs at trying to login in to any of the 9/10 emails.
  2. When I can get a support person to check, they tell me that IMAP and POP3 are turned off.
  3. They will tell me they re-enabled it. But it doesn't enable for me.
  4. It all works OK when using the online webmail Zimbra. However, I need to check 3 of the 9 email accounts every hour, others every 6 hours, and others once a day at boot.

QUESTION: When using POP3, does the setting on my SMTP need a special setting on Thunderbird?.

NOTE: I am complying with "Rogers With Shaw" passing the buck to the Email Client (Thunderbird) by asking here - "Could It Be The Email Client'! And my answer is 'No', because Thunderbird works perfectly with 54 other Email accounts.

Thank for reading this, and for any input you may add, ~r

P.S., I'd like to see a upgrade to this input form that allows us to Preview out Post And, when I started this Post, it alerady had a image from a previous Post
